frameworks.name: bounded accumulation decision model
frameworks.one_line: evidence accumulates to a decision bound that can be adjusted trial-by-trial

frameworks.name: decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: internal correctness estimate used to update subsequent decision policy

frameworks.name: speed-accuracy tradeoff
frameworks.one_line: policy knob adjusted by previous-trial confidence rather than objective outcome

frameworks.name: centro-parietal EEG component
frameworks.one_line: neural signature tracking confidence that predicts next-trial bound shifts

core_question: Do agents use internally computed decision confidence to adjust the speed-accuracy tradeoff on subsequent decisions when external feedback is absent?
key_assumptions: decisions arise from bounded evidence accumulation
confidence is internally computed in absence of feedback
decision bound can be set trial-by-trial
low confidence motivates gathering more evidence next time
manipulations: reported confidence on the previous trial
fitting bounded accumulation model to behavior
key_findings: decision bound on a trial depended on previous-trial reported confidence
this confidence-dependent bound shift was independent of objective accuracy
a centro-parietal EEG component sensitive to confidence predicted the bound increase
interpretations: the brain uses internal confidence signals to continuously adjust decision policies
speed-accuracy tradeoff is regulated by metacognitive estimates rather than external feedback
